evals.prompt: I just published my plugin to SkillStack and want to verify everything is set up correctly. My plugin is 'analytics-pro' at version 2.0.0 with a Polar subscription license.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads config files, queries skillstack_list, runs all 7 checks, verifies storefront, and shows a comprehensive status report
expectations: Reads .claude-plugin/marketplace.json for expected state
Reads .claude-plugin/skillstack.json for SkillStack config
Calls skillstack_list MCP tool to get registered plugins
Checks plugin registration (slug found in list)
Checks version match (source vs SkillStack)
Checks license model correctness
Checks license options sync
Checks creator_contact status
Verifies storefront at store.skillstack.sh
Presents a formatted status report with all check results

evals.prompt: Run /verify on my plugin repo. I think the webhook didn't fire — my local version is 2.1.0 but SkillStack might still show 2.0.0.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects version mismatch and provides specific troubleshooting guidance
expectations: Detects version mismatch between source and SkillStack
Reports the specific version numbers (source vs SkillStack)
Suggests pushing a commit to re-trigger the webhook
Provides the empty commit command as a fix option

evals.prompt: Verify my plugin. I have a freemium plugin with free_skills set to ['write-note', 'hok', 'title'] but the actual skill directories are write-note, hook, and title.
evals.expected_output: Skill validates free_skills against actual directories and catches the typo
expectations: Reads the skills/ directory to get actual skill directory names
Compares each free_skills entry against actual directories
Detects that 'hok' does not match any skill directory
Suggests 'hook' as the closest match for 'hok'
Reports which entries are valid and which are invalid

evals.prompt: Run /verify. I don't have a skillstack.json file yet — I only have marketplace.json.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects missing skillstack.json and directs to /publish
expectations: Reads marketplace.json successfully
Detects that skillstack.json does not exist
Directs the creator to run /publish first to connect plugins to SkillStack
Does NOT attempt to query SkillStack or run checks without skillstack.json

evals.prompt: Run /verify. My marketplace.json still has license_provider and license_config fields from the old format before the skillstack.json sidecar split.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects stale SkillStack fields in marketplace.json and recommends cleanup
expectations: Scans marketplace.json for SkillStack-specific fields
Detects stale fields (license_provider, license_config, etc.)
Reports which fields need to be moved to skillstack.json
Recommends running /publish to migrate automatically
Reports field separation status clearly
